tool.name: claim_target
tool.description:
Claim an endpoint/parameter for testing to prevent duplicate work.
BEFORE testing any endpoint or parameter, claim it so other agents know you're working on it and won't duplicate your effort.
Example workflow: 1. You find /api/users endpoint 2. claim_target("/api/users", "sqli") 3. Perform SQLi testing 4. share_finding() if you find something 5. release_claim() when done

parameters.parameter: The target to claim (URL path, parameter, or feature) Examples: "/api/users", "id parameter on /items", "/login SQLi", "admin panel auth"
Type of test being performed. Examples: "sqli", "xss", "auth_bypass", "idor", "ssrf", "recon", "fuzzing"
Detailed description of what you're testing
How long you expect the test to take. Default: 30

tool.returns: - success: Whether the claim was successful - claim_id: Unique ID for the claim (use for release_claim) - conflict: Info about existing claim if failed
tool.usage_example: <function name="claim_target"> <parameter name="target">/api/users</parameter> <parameter name="test_type">sqli</parameter> <parameter name="description">Testing user search for SQL injection</parameter> <parameter name="estimated_duration_minutes">45</parameter> </function>

tool.name: release_claim
tool.description:
Release a claim on a target, allowing other agents to test it.
Call this when you've finished testing or want to let another agent handle it.

parameters.parameter: The claim ID from claim_target response
Alternative: target name (use with test_type)
Alternative: test type (use with target)
Reason for releasing. Examples: "completed", "blocked", "needs_different_approach"

tool.usage_example:
<!-- Release by claim_id --> <function name="release_claim"> <parameter name="claim_id">claim_abc12345</parameter> <parameter name="reason">completed</parameter> </function>
<!-- Release by target/type --> <function name="release_claim"> <parameter name="target">/api/users</parameter> <parameter name="test_type">sqli</parameter> </function>

tool.name: list_claims
tool.description:
List all current claims to see what's being tested.
Use this to: - Find available targets (not claimed) - See what other agents are working on - Avoid duplicate testing

parameters.parameter: Filter by agent ID or name
Filter by test type (e.g., "sqli", "xss")
Include expired claims. Default: false

tool.returns: - claims: List of active claims with agent info - my_claims: Your own claims - available_targets: Suggestions for unclaimed targets

tool.name: share_finding
tool.description:
Share a vulnerability finding with all agents for potential chaining.
When you discover a vulnerability, SHARE IT so other agents can: - Avoid duplicating your discovery - Chain it with their findings for higher impact - Build upon your work
This is CRITICAL for effective collaboration and vulnerability chaining!

parameters.parameter: Brief title of the finding. Example: "SSRF at /api/fetch"
Type of vulnerability. Examples: "ssrf", "sqli", "xss", "idor", "auth_bypass", "rce", "lfi", "xxe"
Severity level: "critical", "high", "medium", "low", "info"
Where the vulnerability exists (URL, parameter, endpoint)
Detailed description of the vulnerability
Proof-of-concept payload or steps to reproduce
Whether this finding can be chained with others. Default: true
Suggested ways to chain this vulnerability. Example: ["Chain with IDOR for data exfiltration", "Use for internal port scanning"]
Additional tags. Example: ["api", "file_upload", "admin"]

tool.usage_example: <function name="share_finding"> <parameter name="title">SSRF at /api/fetch endpoint</parameter> <parameter name="finding_type">ssrf</parameter> <parameter name="severity">high</parameter> <parameter name="target">/api/fetch?url=</parameter> <parameter name="description">The url parameter allows fetching arbitrary URLs including internal services</parameter> <parameter name="poc">GET /api/fetch?url=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/</parameter> <parameter name="chainable">true</parameter> <parameter name="chain_suggestions">["Chain with internal service access", "Try to access admin panel", "Port scan internal network"]</parameter> </function>

tool.name: list_findings
tool.description:
List shared findings from all agents.
Use this to: - See what vulnerabilities have been discovered - Find chainable vulnerabilities to combine with yours - Avoid duplicate testing - Look for vulnerability chaining opportunities

parameters.parameter: Filter by vulnerability type (e.g., "ssrf", "sqli")
Filter by severity: "critical", "high", "medium", "low"
Only show findings marked as chainable. Default: false
Exclude findings you discovered. Default: false
Maximum results to return. Default: 50

tool.returns: - findings: List of shared findings - chain_opportunities: Suggested vulnerability chains - by_severity: Findings grouped by severity

tool.name: get_finding_details
tool.description: Get full details of a specific finding including the PoC.

parameters.parameter: The finding ID to retrieve

tool.name: add_to_work_queue
tool.description:
Add an endpoint/parameter to the work queue for testing.
Use this to add targets that need testing but you can't handle right now. Other agents can pick up items from the queue.

parameters.parameter: The target to test (URL, endpoint, parameter)
List of test types needed. Example: ["sqli", "xss", "idor"]
Priority level: "critical", "high", "medium", "low". Default: "medium"
Description or notes about the target
Additional context (auth requirements, headers, etc.)

tool.usage_example: <function name="add_to_work_queue"> <parameter name="target">/admin/users</parameter> <parameter name="test_types">["auth_bypass", "idor", "sqli"]</parameter> <parameter name="priority">high</parameter> <parameter name="description">Admin endpoint - requires auth bypass first</parameter> </function>

tool.name: get_next_work_item
tool.description:
Get the next item from the work queue to test.
Use this when you're ready to test something new.

parameters.parameter: List of preferred test types to match
Automatically claim the item. Default: true

tool.name: list_work_queue
tool.description: List items in the work queue.

parameters.parameter: Filter by status: "pending", "claimed", "completed", "all". Default: "all"
Maximum results. Default: 50

tool.name: request_help
tool.description:
Request help from other agents for specialized tasks.
Use this when you encounter something that needs specialized expertise that another agent might be able to help with.

parameters.parameter: Brief title of what you need help with
Detailed description of the problem

Type of help needed: - "analysis": Need help analyzing something (encoded data, obfuscated code) - "exploitation": Need help creating/refining an exploit - "bypass": Need help bypassing a protection (WAF, filter, etc.) - "crypto": Need help with cryptographic analysis - "reversing": Need help with reverse engineering - "other": Other specialized help
The target/endpoint related to the request
How urgent: "low", "medium", "high", "critical". Default: "medium"
Additional data (encoded strings, payloads tried, etc.)

tool.usage_example: <function name="request_help"> <parameter name="title">Base64 encoded parameter - need analysis</parameter> <parameter name="description">Found a parameter with base64 encoding, decoded to what looks like serialized object. Need help identifying the format.</parameter> <parameter name="help_type">analysis</parameter> <parameter name="target">/api/session?data=</parameter> <parameter name="urgency">medium</parameter> <parameter name="attachments">{"encoded_value": "eyJ1c2VyIjoidGVzdCIsInJvbGUiOiJ1c2VyIn0=", "decoded": "{\"user\":\"test\",\"role\":\"user\"}"}</parameter> </function>

tool.name: respond_to_help_request
tool.description: Respond to another agent's help request.

parameters.parameter: The help request ID to respond to
Your response/suggestion
A specific solution if you have one
Whether you're offering to take over this task. Default: false

tool.name: list_help_requests
tool.description: List help requests from all agents.

parameters.parameter: Filter by status: "open", "resolved", "all". Default: "open"
Filter by help type
Include your own requests. Default: true

tool.name: get_collaboration_status
tool.description:
Get an overview of all collaboration activities.
Returns a summary of: - Active claims - Shared findings - Work queue status - Help requests - Recent broadcasts - Vulnerability chain opportunities
Use this to get a quick snapshot of what all agents are doing.
tool.returns: - summary: Counts of claims, findings, queue items, help requests - my_activity: Your claims, findings, work items - chain_opportunities: Suggested vulnerability chains - recommendations: What you should do next

tool.name: broadcast_message
tool.description:
Broadcast a message to all or specific agents.
Use this for important updates that all agents should know about.

parameters.parameter: The message to broadcast
Type: "info", "warning", "alert", "update". Default: "info"
Priority: "low", "normal", "high". Default: "normal"
Specific agent IDs to message. Default: all agents

tool.usage_example: <function name="broadcast_message"> <parameter name="message">Found WAF blocking SQLi payloads - use tamper scripts</parameter> <parameter name="message_type">warning</parameter> <parameter name="priority">high</parameter> </function>
